question: A FEEL output mapping captures reference codes from a free-text field with the expression below. What value does the call return?

a.text: `extract` returns all substrings matching the regular expression. The pattern `12[0-9]*` requires the leading literal `12`, so `1234` and `1256` match but `1378` does not.

b.text: `1378` starts with `13`, not `12`, so it cannot match the anchored prefix `12[0-9]*`; only two matches are returned.

c.text: `extract` always returns a list, even when only one match is found; it never returns a bare string.

d.text: An empty list would only be returned if the pattern did not match anywhere; here two matches are present.

explanation: `extract(string, pattern)` returns the list of all substrings that match the given regular expression. With pattern `12[0-9]*`, only references beginning with the literal characters `12` are matched, so `extract("references are 1234, 1256, 1378", "12[0-9]*")` returns `["1234", "1256"]`.
